binaries.title: mountmgr.sys - No security-relevant change (device-characteristic gate added to remote-database reconciliation)

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00014C0
key_changes: Compiler-generated memset/memfill routine replaced with different optimization variant (SSE/AVX vectorization). No behavioral change.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0001010
key_changes: IRP cancel routine iteration refactored — loop structure reorganized around cancel spinlock. Control flow simplified but semantics unchanged.

changed_functions.name: MountMgrDriverReinitialization (sub_1C000F2D0 / patched sub_1C000F350)
key_changes: A caller-side 'if (byte at 0x7c == 0)' guard around the call to ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster was removed; the equivalent test now lives inside the callee.
This is a driver reinitialization routine (registered via IoRegisterDriverReinitialization), not an attacker-reachable IOCTL path.
changed_functions.security_notes: Refactor only. The removable-media (0x7c) gate that previously sat at this call site was relocated into ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster, which now also tests the new 0x7d device-characteristic byte. Behavior is preserved; no security impact.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0015B44
key_changes: Device detection logic refactored — large cascaded if/else chain of device signature comparisons (var_d8/var_d0 GUID-like comparisons) reorganized into flattened || conditions.
Branch order swapped: the 'rax_8 == rbx_1' device-type path and the 'rax_8 == 0' path swapped evaluation order.
memset size changed from 0x90 to 0x8c for var_f8 (minor structural change).

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000F390
key_changes: List iteration refactored — loop that searches for an entry with flag 0x100 restructured for clarity. Semantics unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0016760
key_changes: Buffer allocation retry loop refactored — control flow simplified around ExAllocatePoolWithTag + IOCTL query. Error path cleanup restructured. No semantic change to memory handling.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0002DF0
key_changes: Worker thread dispatch loop refactored — event wait loop restructured. Global data references changed (data_1c00061d0 → data_1c0006220, data_1c00061f0 → data_1c0006250) reflecting version-specific data layout differences.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0019120
key_changes: Control flow refactored with no semantic change.

changed_functions.name: ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker (sub_1C00170E0 / patched sub_1C0017130)
key_changes: The work-item callback re-validates its volume entry under the device mutex: it acquires the mutex at Context+56 and calls MntMgrFindDevice (0x1C000E1A4), which walks the device list and returns the entry only if it is still linked, then bails if the entry is not found or its 0x7c byte is set. This re-lookup is present and identical in BOTH builds.
The only delta: the patched bail-out condition additionally tests the new 0x7d device-characteristic byte (Device == head || byte 0x7c != 0 || byte 0x7d != 0).
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a UAF sink. The mutex-protected MntMgrFindDevice re-lookup that guards against a freed/unlinked entry exists unchanged in both builds. The only change is the added 0x7d test, mirroring the queue-side filter. 0x7d is a static device characteristic (Characteristics bit 0x10000) latched once in QueryDeviceInformation, not a teardown flag, so it cannot be a race guard.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00183D4
key_changes: Linked list removal and reinsertion logic refactored — control flow restructured but list manipulation semantics preserved. Added trap(0xd) assertion guard.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000D898
key_changes: Buffer allocation retry loop refactored for clarity. Error path restructured with early returns. No semantic change.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00152E4
key_changes: Volume database file creation logic refactored — IoCreateFile + sub_1c000c878/sub_1c000c8e0 fallback path restructured. Error handling consolidated. No semantic change.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0014C24
key_changes: Large volume processing function refactored. Control flow restructured but no new security checks added or removed.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0018990
key_changes: Integer overflow protection: '*arg4 + 2' computed as r14_1 in unpatched, moved to inline r14+2 in patched. Semantics identical. Loop and IOCTL dispatch structure unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00162A0
key_changes: Registry query and volume list building refactored — error path consolidated, cleanup loop restructured. No semantic change.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000E458
key_changes: Volume/symbolic link matching logic refactored — loop restructured with while(true) + break pattern. Semantics unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000C878
key_changes: Work item dispatch helper refactored — error path restructured with label consolidation. Memory handling (ExAllocatePoolWithTag/ExFreePoolWithTag) semantics unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0009E90
key_changes: Large processing function refactored — control flow restructured.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0015EBC
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0010D18
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C001085C
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: MountMgrMountedDeviceArrival (sub_1C000F7E4 / patched relocated)
key_changes: Mounted-device arrival notification handler; it examines newly arrived devices and can call ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster.
Differences between builds are relocation noise plus the mechanical +1 field-offset shift from the inserted 0x7d byte; no added or removed check.
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a mounted-device arrival notification handler, not a raw IOCTL dispatcher. It shows no logic change of its own; the only behavioral effect it inherits is that ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster now filters an additional device class. No security impact.

changed_functions.name: ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster (sub_1C0016FBC / patched sub_1C0016FFC)
key_changes: Added a second device-property test before queuing the reconcile work item: unpatched tests only byte 0x7c; patched tests byte 0x7c AND the new byte 0x7d, bailing if either is non-zero.

Both bytes are set once in QueryDeviceInformation from the target device object's Characteristics: 0x7c = Characteristics & 1 (FILE_REMOVABLE_MEDIA), 0x7d = BYTE2(Characteristics) & 1 (bit 0x10000). 0x7d is a new field, present only in the patched build.

Assembly: unpatched at 0x1c0016fcb tests 0x7c then allocates. Patched at 0x1c001700b tests 0x7c, at 0x1c001701b tests 0x7d, then allocates. The 0x38-byte NonPagedPoolNx 'MntA' allocation is the work-item context, not the volume entry.
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix. The added 0x7d byte is a static device characteristic (Characteristics bit 0x10000) latched once when the device is examined and never mutated, so it cannot guard against a concurrent free/teardown. The change is a behavioral filter that excludes one class of device from remote-database reconciliation and from the follow-on OnlineMountedVolumes call. The work-item callback (ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker) independently re-validates the entry under the device mutex via MntMgrFindDevice in BOTH builds, which is the actual protection against a stale entry pointer.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED sub_1C0016FBC entry:
0x1c0016fcb: cmp byte [rdx+0x7c], 0x0    ; tests 0x7c (FILE_REMOVABLE_MEDIA)
0x1c0016fcf: mov rsi, rdx
0x1c0016fd2: mov rbx, rcx
0x1c0016fd5: jne 0x1c00170c5               ; if 0x7c != 0, epilogue
0x1c0016fdb: mov edx, 0x38                 ; work-item context size

PATCHED sub_1C0016FFC entry:
0x1c001700b: cmp byte [rdx+0x7c], 0x0    ; tests 0x7c
0x1c001700f: mov rsi, rdx
0x1c0017012: mov rbx, rcx
0x1c0017015: jne 0x1c001710f               ; if 0x7c != 0, epilogue
0x1c001701b: cmp byte [rdx+0x7d], 0x0    ; NEW: tests 0x7d (Characteristics bit 0x10000)
0x1c001701f: jne 0x1c001710f               ; if 0x7d != 0, epilogue
0x1c0017025: mov edx, 0x38                 ; work-item context size

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00091D0
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00110C8
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00185C0
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000E124
key_changes: Variable renames and loop restructuring only.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000DDC0
key_changes: Variable renames only — IoCreateSymbolicLink path unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C00157D0
key_changes: Variable renames only.

key_changes: Variable renames only.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C000CA20
key_changes: Variable renames only — work item callback unchanged.

changed_functions.name: sub_1C0013F14
key_changes: Control flow refactored.

security_findings.entry_point: Mounted-device arrival notification (MountMgrMountedDeviceArrival, sub_1C000F7E4) and driver reinitialization (MountMgrDriverReinitialization, sub_1C000F2D0), among other internal callers of ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster.
call_chain: ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker (callback; re-validates the entry under the device mutex via MntMgrFindDevice)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: N/A — behavioral change, no demonstrable security impact
security_findings.description: The patched ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster adds a second device-property test before it queues the reconcile work item: the unpatched build tests only the byte at offset 0x7c (Characteristics & 1, FILE_REMOVABLE_MEDIA), while the patched build additionally tests the byte at offset 0x7d and bails if either is non-zero. Both bytes are set once, in QueryDeviceInformation (0x1C00157D0 / 0x1C00157F8), directly from the target device object's Characteristics field; 0x7d = BYTE2(Characteristics) & 1 (bit 0x10000) and is a new field in the patched build (its insertion shifted subsequent fields up by one byte). Because 0x7d is a static device characteristic latched at examination time and never mutated, it cannot function as a concurrent-teardown guard. The change is a behavioral filter that excludes one class of device from remote-database reconciliation and from the follow-on OnlineMountedVolumes call. This is NOT a use-after-free fix: the work-item callback ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker already re-validates the entry under the device mutex (KeWaitForSingleObject at Context+56) by calling MntMgrFindDevice (0x1C000E1A4), which returns the entry only if it is still linked in the device list, and this re-validation is present and unchanged in BOTH builds.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. No vulnerable condition exists in either build along this path. The only observable difference is that the patched build skips the reconcile work item and OnlineMountedVolumes for devices whose target device object Characteristics have bit 0x10000 set.
security_findings.primitive: None. The change frees no memory, extends no lifetime past a free, and relaxes no bound, lock, or access check.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster @ 0x1C0016FBC:
  if ( *(_BYTE *)(a2 + 124) == 0 ) { /* allocate 0x38-byte NonPagedPoolNx 'MntA' work-item context; queue ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker */ }

PATCHED ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster @ 0x1C0016FFC:
  if ( *(_WORD *)(a2 + 124) == 0 ) { /* identical body; folds the 0x7c and new 0x7d byte tests into one 16-bit compare */ }

Field source, PATCHED QueryDeviceInformation @ 0x1C00157F8:
  *(_BYTE *)(a2 + 124) = v4->Characteristics & 1;          // 0x7c (unchanged)
  *(_BYTE *)(a2 + 125) = BYTE2(v4->Characteristics) & 1;   // 0x7d (NEW field, Characteristics bit 0x10000)

Callback re-validation (present in BOTH builds), ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker:
  KeWaitForSingleObject((PVOID)(v1 + 56), ...);            // device mutex
  Device = MntMgrFindDevice(v1, (_QWORD *)v2);             // returns entry only if still linked
  if ( Device == <list head> || *(_BYTE *)(v2 + 124) != 0 [ || *(_BYTE *)(v2 + 125) != 0 in patched ] ) { release mutex; return; }
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED sub_1C0016FBC entry:
0x1c0016fcb: cmp byte [rdx+0x7c], 0x0     ; tests 0x7c (FILE_REMOVABLE_MEDIA)
0x1c0016fd5: jne 0x1c00170c5              ; if 0x7c != 0, epilogue
0x1c0016fdb: mov edx, 0x38                ; work-item context size
0x1c0016fe0: mov ecx, 0x200               ; NonPagedPoolNx
0x1c0016fe5: mov r8d, 0x41746e4d          ; tag 'MntA'
0x1c0016feb: call __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
0x1c0017085: mov [rdi+0x30], rsi          ; stores entry pointer into work-item context
0x1c001708d: mov [rdi+0x18], rax          ; stores ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker callback
0x1c001709a: call QueueWorkItem

PATCHED sub_1C0016FFC entry:
0x1c001700b: cmp byte [rdx+0x7c], 0x0     ; tests 0x7c
0x1c0017015: jne 0x1c001710f              ; if 0x7c != 0, epilogue
0x1c001701b: cmp byte [rdx+0x7d], 0x0     ; NEW: tests 0x7d (Characteristics bit 0x10000)
0x1c001701f: jne 0x1c001710f              ; if 0x7d != 0, epilogue
0x1c0017025: mov edx, 0x38                ; identical allocation and queue path follows
security_findings.notes: The 0x38-byte NonPagedPoolNx 'MntA' allocation in this function is the work-item context structure, not the volume entry; only the entry's address is stored into the context. The volume entry's own pool type and size are not determinable from this function. Two unrelated changes are bundled in the same build and are not security-relevant: GlobalCreateSymbolicLink (0x1C000DDC0 / 0x1C000DE30) switched from IoCreateSymbolicLink to IoCreateSymbolicLink2, and QueryDeviceSortOrderInternal (0x1C0015B44 / 0x1C0015B78) updated its device-signature GUID comparison table.

summary: No security-relevant change. The patched ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMaster adds a second device-property test (byte 0x7d) before queuing its remote-database reconcile work item, and the callback ReconcileThisDatabaseWithMasterWorker adds the same test to its bail-out condition. Both 0x7c and 0x7d are set once in QueryDeviceInformation directly from the target device object's Characteristics field (0x7c = FILE_REMOVABLE_MEDIA; 0x7d = bit 0x10000, a new field), so 0x7d is a static device characteristic, not a dynamic teardown flag, and cannot guard against a concurrent free. The change is a behavioral filter that excludes one class of device from database reconciliation and volume onlining. The actual protection against a stale entry pointer is the callback's mutex-protected MntMgrFindDevice re-lookup, which is present and unchanged in both builds. The earlier UAF/CWE-416/CWE-362 characterization is not supported by the binaries. Two unrelated changes are bundled: an IoCreateSymbolicLink -> IoCreateSymbolicLink2 API switch in GlobalCreateSymbolicLink, and a device-signature GUID table update in QueryDeviceSortOrderInternal; neither shows a security impact.
